# DaisyUI Prompt Kit

`@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit` is a React component library for building AI chat interfaces on top of DaisyUI.

It is designed for DaisyUI >= 5.0, React >= 19.0, and Tailwind CSS >= 4.0.

This package is a DaisyUI-native alternative to the original shadcn-based prompt-kit. Do not introduce shadcn components or Radix primitives when using this library.

---

## Quick Start

### 1. Install the package

```bash
npm install @turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit
# or
pnpm add @turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit
```

### 2. Set up peer dependencies

#### Required

Your project must already have these installed (standard DaisyUI setup):

```bash
npm install react react-dom tailwindcss daisyui
```

#### Recommended (for full functionality)

These are optional peer dependencies. Most components with built-in icons require Iconify, and markdown-based components require the typography plugin.

```bash
npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography @iconify/tailwind4 @iconify-json/lucide
```

| Package | Purpose | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| `@tailwindcss/typography` | `prose` classes for rendering styled HTML from markdown | `Markdown`, `MessageContent`, `ReasoningContent` |
| `@iconify/tailwind4` | Renders Lucide icons via Tailwind CSS classes (`icon-[lucide--*]`) | `CodeBlockCopyButton`, `ScrollButton`, `Tool`, `SystemMessage`, `FeedbackBar`, `ThinkingBar`, `Reasoning`, `ChainOfThought`, `Steps` |
| `@iconify-json/lucide` | Lucide icon data for `@iconify/tailwind4` | Same as above |

> **Without `@tailwindcss/typography`:** `Markdown`, `MessageContent`, and `ReasoningContent` will render unstyled HTML (no heading sizes, no list bullets, no link colors).
>
> **Without `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`:** Components with built-in icons (copy button, scroll chevron, feedback thumbs, system message status icons, etc.) will show empty placeholders instead of icons.

### 3. Configure your CSS

In your global CSS entry file (e.g., `globals.css` or `app.css`), add the necessary plugins:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

/* Enable icons from the library's built-in icon classes */
@plugin "@iconify/tailwind4";

/* Enable prose styling for markdown rendering */
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";

/* DaisyUI with your preferred themes */
@plugin "daisyui" {
  themes: light --default, dark --prefersdark;
}
```

#### Important: Tailwind CSS 4 source scanning (REQUIRED)

The library does NOT ship Tailwind CSS or DaisyUI — it only ships ~3 KB of custom animations/keyframes.
All utility classes are resolved by your project's own Tailwind build. You MUST add a `@source` directive
so Tailwind scans the library's JS files and generates the required utility classes:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@source "../node_modules/@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit/dist/**/*.js";
```

Without this directive, many library classes (e.g., `hidden`, `sr-only`, `animate-*`, `prose-*`) will be missing.

### 4. Import and use

```tsx
import {
  ChatContainerRoot,
  ChatContainerContent,
  Message,
  MessageAvatar,
  MessageContent,
  PromptInput,
  PromptInputTextarea,
  PromptInputActions,
  PromptInputAction,
  ScrollButton,
} from '@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit'
```

#### Styles

Import the library's custom styles (animations, keyframes, markdown formatting):

```tsx
import '@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit/styles'
```

If you use math rendering (KaTeX), also import:

```tsx
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css'
```

---

## Core Rules

1. Use DaisyUI component classes and Tailwind utility classes only.
2. Do not add shadcn primitives or Radix wrappers around these components.
3. Prefer composing the exported primitives instead of rewriting the same UI from scratch.
4. For theme consistency, use DaisyUI semantic colors like `primary`, `secondary`, `base-100`, and `base-content`.

---

## Components

### PromptInput

The chat composer shell. Provides context to child components for auto-resize, submit handling, and loading state.

#### Exports

- `PromptInput` — Outer container with rounded border and shadow
- `PromptInputTextarea` — Auto-resizing textarea
- `PromptInputActions` — Action button row
- `PromptInputAction` — Single action button wrapper with tooltip

#### Example

```tsx
<PromptInput value={value} onValueChange={setValue} onSubmit={handleSubmit} isLoading={isSending}>
  <PromptInputTextarea placeholder="Type your message" />
  <PromptInputActions className="justify-end">
    <PromptInputAction tooltip="Send">
      <button type="button" className="btn btn-primary btn-sm btn-circle">
        <span className="icon-[lucide--arrow-up] size-4" />
      </button>
    </PromptInputAction>
  </PromptInputActions>
</PromptInput>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PromptInput.value` | `string` | `''` | Controlled text value |
| `PromptInput.onValueChange` | `(value: string) => void` | — | Called when text changes |
| `PromptInput.onSubmit` | `() => void` | — | Called on Enter key |
| `PromptInput.isLoading` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether a request is in progress |
| `PromptInput.maxHeight` | `number \| string` | `240` | Max textarea height before scrolling |
| `PromptInput.disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables input |
| `PromptInputTextarea.disableAutosize` | `boolean` | `false` | Turns off auto-resize |
| `PromptInputAction.tooltip` | `ReactNode` | — | Tooltip content |
| `PromptInputAction.side` | `'top' \| 'bottom' \| 'left' \| 'right'` | `'top'` | Tooltip position |

#### Built-in styling

- Container: `rounded-3xl border border-base-300 bg-base-100 shadow-xs`
- Textarea: borderless, transparent background, dark-mode compatible placeholder
- Enter submits, Shift+Enter inserts newline
- Click on the container focuses the textarea

#### Integration notes

- `PromptInputAction` wraps your own button/label/icon; it does not render a button itself.
- The consumer provides send/stop/attach icons — the library does not ship these as defaults because they are application-specific.

---

### Message

Renders user or assistant chat messages with optional markdown, avatar, and action buttons.

#### Exports

- `Message` — Flex container for a single message
- `MessageAvatar` — Round avatar with image or fallback initial
- `MessageContent` — Content bubble with optional markdown rendering
- `MessageActions` — Action button row
- `MessageAction` — Single action button with tooltip

#### Example

```tsx
{/* User message — right-aligned, colored bubble */}
<Message variant="user">
  <MessageContent variant="user">Hello, how can you help?</MessageContent>
</Message>

{/* Assistant message — left-aligned, transparent background */}
<Message variant="assistant">
  <MessageAvatar fallback="AI" />
  <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
    <MessageContent variant="assistant" markdown>
      {'I can help with **coding**, research, and more.'}
    </MessageContent>
    <MessageActions>
      <MessageAction tooltip="Copy">
        <button className="btn btn-ghost btn-sm btn-circle">
          <span className="icon-[lucide--copy] size-4" />
        </button>
      </MessageAction>
    </MessageActions>
  </div>
</Message>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Message.variant` | `'user' \| 'assistant'` | — | Sets alignment (`justify-end` for user, `justify-start` for assistant) |
| `MessageContent.variant` | `'user' \| 'assistant'` | — | Sets style: user = `bg-secondary p-2`, assistant = `bg-transparent p-0` |
| `MessageContent.markdown` | `boolean` | `false` | When true, renders content through `<Markdown>` |
| `MessageAvatar.src` | `string` | — | Avatar image URL |
| `MessageAvatar.fallback` | `string` | — | Fallback initial when no image |
| `MessageAction.tooltip` | `ReactNode` | — | Tooltip content |

#### Built-in styling

- `MessageContent` includes `prose prose-sm max-w-none` — no need to add prose classes yourself
- User variant: `bg-secondary p-2 rounded-lg`
- Assistant variant: `bg-transparent p-0` — clean text without a bubble
- Without `variant`, defaults to `bg-secondary p-2` (backward compatible)

#### Requires

- `@tailwindcss/typography` — for `prose` classes used internally

---

### Markdown

Renders markdown strings into styled HTML with support for GFM, math (KaTeX), and syntax-highlighted code blocks.

#### Example

```tsx
<Markdown>
  {`# Hello World\n\nThis is **bold** and $E = mc^2$ is math.`}
</Markdown>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `children` | `string` | — | Markdown content |
| `className` | `string` | — | Additional classes (merged with defaults) |
| `components` | `Partial<Components>` | — | Override react-markdown components |

#### Built-in styling

The component applies these classes by default — you do NOT need to add `prose` manually:

```
prose prose-h1:text-2xl prose-h2:text-xl prose-h3:text-lg prose-h4:text-base prose-h5:text-sm prose-h6:text-xs max-w-none
```

You can override or extend by passing `className`:

```tsx
<Markdown className="prose-lg dark:prose-invert">
  {content}
</Markdown>
```

#### Built-in features

- Headings, lists, tables, blockquotes (via `remark-gfm`)
- Inline and block math equations (via `remark-math` + `rehype-katex`)
- Fenced code blocks auto-rendered with `CodeBlock` + `CodeBlockCode` (Shiki syntax highlighting)
- Inline code rendered as pill-style `bg-base-200 rounded-sm px-1 font-mono text-sm`
- KaTeX CSS must be imported separately: `import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css'`

#### Requires

- `@tailwindcss/typography` — for `prose` classes

---

### CodeBlock

Displays code with syntax highlighting (Shiki), optional header bar, and built-in copy button.

#### Exports

- `CodeBlock` — Container with border and rounded corners
- `CodeBlockCode` — Syntax-highlighted code content
- `CodeBlockGroup` — Header bar for title/language/actions
- `CodeBlockCopyButton` — Built-in copy-to-clipboard button with icon swap

#### Example

```tsx
<CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlockGroup>
    <span className="text-xs text-base-content/60">tsx</span>
    <CodeBlockCopyButton code={sourceCode} />
  </CodeBlockGroup>
  <CodeBlockCode code={sourceCode} language="tsx" />
</CodeBlock>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CodeBlockCode.code` | `string` | — | Source code to display |
| `CodeBlockCode.language` | `string` | `'tsx'` | Language for syntax highlighting |
| `CodeBlockCode.theme` | `string` | auto-detected | Shiki theme (auto-switches light/dark) |
| `CodeBlockCopyButton.code` | `string` | — | Code to copy to clipboard |
| `CodeBlockCopyButton.copiedTimeout` | `number` | `2000` | Time (ms) before icon resets |

#### Built-in features

- `CodeBlockCopyButton` handles all clipboard logic, renders `icon-[lucide--copy]` / `icon-[lucide--check]` automatically
- `CodeBlockCode` auto-detects light/dark theme from `data-theme`, `color-scheme`, or `prefers-color-scheme`
- `CodeBlockGroup` provides a `border-b border-base-300 px-4 py-2` header row
- `CodeBlock` has `not-prose` class to prevent prose styling from interfering

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide` — for copy/check icons in `CodeBlockCopyButton`

---

### ChatContainer

Provides a scrollable transcript region with auto-scroll-to-bottom behavior.

#### Exports

- `ChatContainerRoot` — Scroll container with `use-stick-to-bottom`
- `ChatContainerContent` — Vertical message stack
- `ChatContainerScrollAnchor` — Optional explicit scroll target

#### Example

```tsx
<ChatContainerRoot className="h-[560px] rounded-box border border-base-300 bg-base-100">
  <ChatContainerContent className="gap-4 p-4">
    {messages.map((msg) => (
      <Message key={msg.id} variant={msg.role}>
        <MessageContent variant={msg.role} markdown>{msg.content}</MessageContent>
      </Message>
    ))}
  </ChatContainerContent>
</ChatContainerRoot>
```

---

### ScrollButton

A floating button that scrolls the chat to the latest content. Must be used inside `ChatContainerRoot`.

#### Example

```tsx
<div className="absolute bottom-4 right-4">
  <ScrollButton variant="primary" size="md" />
</div>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'ghost' \| 'outline'` | `'outline'` |
| `size` | `'xs' \| 'sm' \| 'md'` | `'sm'` |

- Auto-hides when already at bottom
- Built-in `icon-[lucide--chevron-down]`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### Reasoning

Collapsible model reasoning trace with built-in left border indicator.

#### Exports

- `Reasoning` — Context provider with open/close state
- `ReasoningTrigger` — Toggle button with chevron
- `ReasoningContent` — Collapsible content area

#### Example

```tsx
<Reasoning isStreaming={isThinking}>
  <ReasoningTrigger>Show reasoning</ReasoningTrigger>
  <ReasoningContent markdown>{reasoningText}</ReasoningContent>
</Reasoning>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Reasoning.isStreaming` | `boolean` | — | Auto-opens when true, auto-closes when false |
| `Reasoning.open` | `boolean` | — | Controlled open state |
| `ReasoningContent.markdown` | `boolean` | `false` | Render content as markdown |
| `ReasoningContent.contentClassName` | `string` | — | Additional classes on inner content |

#### Built-in styling

- Content area: `ml-2 border-l-2 border-base-300 px-2 pb-1` — the left border indicator is built in
- Text: `prose prose-sm max-w-none text-base-content/70`
- Trigger: `text-primary` with animated `icon-[lucide--chevron-down]`
- Smooth grid-based collapse animation

#### Requires

- `@tailwindcss/typography`, `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### ResponseStream

Progressively reveals text using typewriter or fade animation.

#### Example

```tsx
<ResponseStream textStream={responseText} mode="typewriter" speed={20} />
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `textStream` | `string \| AsyncIterable<string>` | — |
| `mode` | `'typewriter' \| 'fade'` | `'typewriter'` |
| `speed` | `number` | `20` |
| `as` | `keyof JSX.IntrinsicElements` | `'div'` |
| `onComplete` | `() => void` | — |

---

### PromptSuggestion

Suggestion chips for starter prompts and quick actions. Two modes: pill buttons and highlighted list items.

#### Example

```tsx
<div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
  <PromptSuggestion onClick={() => setValue('Tell me a joke')}>Tell me a joke</PromptSuggestion>
  <PromptSuggestion highlight="How to">How to create a React component</PromptSuggestion>
</div>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'outline' \| 'ghost'` | — |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | — |
| `highlight` | `string` | — |

---

### FileUpload

File attachment with drag-and-drop overlay. Provides context for trigger and overlay components.

#### Exports

- `FileUpload` — Context provider with file callbacks
- `FileUploadTrigger` — Button that opens the file picker
- `FileUploadContent` — Portal overlay shown during drag

#### Example

```tsx
<FileUpload onFilesAdded={handleFiles} accept="image/*,.pdf">
  <FileUploadTrigger className="btn btn-outline btn-sm">Attach</FileUploadTrigger>
  <FileUploadContent className="rounded-box p-3">
    <p className="text-sm text-base-content/70">Drop files here</p>
  </FileUploadContent>
</FileUpload>
```

---

### Tool

Displays tool calls with status indicators, input/output payloads, and error details. Self-contained — no extra styling needed.

#### Example

```tsx
<Tool
  toolPart={{
    type: 'web_search',
    state: 'output-available',
    toolCallId: 'call_1',
    input: { query: 'daisyui modal docs' },
    output: { results: 3 },
  }}
/>
```

#### Built-in features

- State icons: spinning loader, gear, check, error X — all built in
- State badges with semantic colors (info, warning, success, error)
- Collapsible body showing input, output, and error payloads
- Formatted JSON display

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### Source

Citation links with hoverable preview cards. Fetches favicon automatically.

#### Exports

- `Source` — Context provider with URL
- `SourceTrigger` — Clickable citation chip
- `SourceContent` — Hover card with title and description

#### Example

```tsx
<Source href="https://daisyui.com/components/modal/">
  <SourceTrigger showFavicon />
  <SourceContent title="Modal" description="DaisyUI modal component docs." />
</Source>
```

---

### ChainOfThought

Multi-step collapsible reasoning traces with vertical timeline.

#### Example

```tsx
<ChainOfThought>
  <ChainOfThoughtStep>
    <ChainOfThoughtTrigger leftIcon={<span className="icon-[lucide--search] size-4" />}>
      Analyzing request
    </ChainOfThoughtTrigger>
    <ChainOfThoughtContent>
      <ChainOfThoughtItem>Identify user intent</ChainOfThoughtItem>
      <ChainOfThoughtItem>Find implementation details</ChainOfThoughtItem>
    </ChainOfThoughtContent>
  </ChainOfThoughtStep>
</ChainOfThought>
```

#### Built-in features

- Vertical timeline connector lines (`bg-primary/20`)
- `swapIconOnHover` — custom left icon swaps to chevron on hover
- Auto-marks last step for clean timeline ending
- Built-in `icon-[lucide--chevron-down]`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### Steps

Collapsible multi-step agent workflow traces with vertical bar.

#### Example

```tsx
<Steps defaultOpen>
  <StepsTrigger>Agent run: Summarize repository</StepsTrigger>
  <StepsContent>
    <StepsItem>Searching files...</StepsItem>
    <StepsItem>Parsing markdown</StepsItem>
    <StepsItem className="text-base-content">Summary generated</StepsItem>
  </StepsContent>
</Steps>
```

#### Built-in features

- Vertical bar (`StepsBar`), customizable via `bar` prop
- Grid-based collapse animation
- `swapIconOnHover` on `StepsTrigger`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### FeedbackBar

Thumbs up/down and close actions for assistant messages. Self-contained.

#### Example

```tsx
<FeedbackBar
  title="Was this helpful?"
  onHelpful={handleUpvote}
  onNotHelpful={handleDownvote}
  onClose={handleDismiss}
/>
```

#### Built-in icons

- `icon-[lucide--thumbs-up]`, `icon-[lucide--thumbs-down]`, `icon-[lucide--x]`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### SystemMessage

Status banners for info, success, warning, and error messages.

#### Example

```tsx
<SystemMessage variant="warning">Model response is still streaming.</SystemMessage>
<SystemMessage variant="success" fill>Operation completed successfully.</SystemMessage>
<SystemMessage variant="error" cta={{ label: 'Retry', onClick: handleRetry }}>
  Failed to generate response.
</SystemMessage>
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `variant` | `'info' \| 'success' \| 'warning' \| 'error'` | `'info'` | Message type |
| `fill` | `boolean` | `false` | Filled background vs outlined border |
| `icon` | `ReactNode` | — | Override default icon |
| `isIconHidden` | `boolean` | `false` | Hide icon entirely |
| `cta` | `{ label: string; onClick?: () => void }` | — | CTA button on the right |

#### Built-in features

- Default icons per variant (info, check, warning, alert)
- Filled mode: `bg-{variant}/10 border-transparent`
- Outlined mode: `border-{variant}/40`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### ThinkingBar

Animated label showing the model is thinking, with optional stop button.

#### Example

```tsx
<ThinkingBar isThinking text="Analyzing files..." onStop={handleStop} />
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `isThinking` | `boolean` | `true` | Show/hide the bar |
| `text` | `string` | `'Thinking'` | Animated label text |
| `onStop` | `() => void` | — | Renders a stop button when provided |
| `stopLabel` | `string` | `'Answer now'` | Stop button text |
| `onClick` | `() => void` | — | Makes the label clickable |

#### Built-in features

- Uses `TextShimmer` for animated text
- Returns `null` when `isThinking` is false
- Built-in `icon-[lucide--chevron-right]`

#### Requires

- `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide`

---

### Loader

Animated loading indicators with 12 built-in variants. Fully self-contained — no external dependencies.

#### Example

```tsx
<Loader variant="typing" size="md" />
<Loader variant="text-shimmer" text="Generating..." />
```

#### Props

| Prop | Type | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `variant` | `'circular' \| 'classic' \| 'pulse' \| 'pulse-dot' \| 'dots' \| 'typing' \| 'wave' \| 'bars' \| 'terminal' \| 'text-blink' \| 'text-shimmer' \| 'loading-dots'` | `'circular'` |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` |
| `text` | `string` | — |

---

### TextShimmer

Lightweight animated text emphasis for loading labels.

#### Example

```tsx
<TextShimmer as="p" duration={4} spread={20}>Loading amazing content...</TextShimmer>
```

---

### Image

Image component with loading skeleton, error fallback, and aspect ratio control. Fully self-contained.

#### Example

```tsx
<Image src="https://picsum.photos/800/450" alt="Preview" aspectRatio="video" />
```

---

### JSXPreview

Runtime JSX renderer for previewing streamed JSX strings.

#### Example

```tsx
<JSXPreview jsx={'<div className="badge badge-primary">Ready</div>'} isStreaming />
```

---

## Hooks

### useTextStream

Low-level hook for progressive text rendering. Used internally by `ResponseStream`.

```tsx
const { displayedText, isComplete, startStreaming, pause, resume } = useTextStream({
  textStream,
  mode: 'typewriter',
  speed: 20,
})
```

#### Returns

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `displayedText` | `string` | Currently visible text |
| `isComplete` | `boolean` | Whether streaming finished |
| `segments` | `Segment[]` | Parsed segments for fade mode |
| `startStreaming()` | `() => void` | Manually restart stream |
| `pause()` | `() => void` | Pause typewriter |
| `resume()` | `() => void` | Resume typewriter |
| `reset()` | `() => void` | Reset to empty |

---

## Utility Exports

### cn

Tailwind class merging utility (wraps `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`):

```tsx
import { cn } from '@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit'

cn('btn btn-primary', isActive && 'btn-active', className)
```

---

## Dependency Map

### What the library bundles (you don't need to install these)

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `react-markdown` | Markdown → React rendering |
| `remark-gfm` | GitHub Flavored Markdown (tables, strikethrough, etc.) |
| `remark-math` + `rehype-katex` | Math equation rendering |
| `katex` | KaTeX CSS (import `katex/dist/katex.min.css` separately) |
| `shiki` | Syntax highlighting for code blocks |
| `marked` | Markdown tokenizer for block splitting |
| `use-stick-to-bottom` | Chat container auto-scroll |
| `react-jsx-parser` | JSX preview runtime rendering |
| `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` | Class name merging |

### What you provide (peer dependencies)

| Package | Required? | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `react` >= 19 | **Yes** | — |
| `react-dom` >= 19 | **Yes** | — |
| `tailwindcss` >= 4 | **Yes** | CSS framework |
| `daisyui` >= 5 | **Yes** | Component classes and theming |
| `@tailwindcss/typography` >= 0.5 | Recommended | `prose` classes for Markdown, MessageContent, ReasoningContent |
| `@iconify/tailwind4` >= 1 | Recommended | Renders built-in Lucide icon classes |
| `@iconify-json/lucide` >= 1 | Recommended | Lucide icon data |

---

## Minimal Integration Example

For a project that already uses DaisyUI 5 + Tailwind CSS 4, here is the minimal setup:

### 1. Install

```bash
pnpm add @turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit
pnpm add -D @tailwindcss/typography @iconify/tailwind4 @iconify-json/lucide
```

### 2. CSS (globals.css)

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "@iconify/tailwind4";
@plugin "@tailwindcss/typography";
@plugin "daisyui" {
  themes: light --default, dark --prefersdark;
}

/* Required: scan library JS so Tailwind generates its utility classes */
@source "../node_modules/@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit/dist/**/*.js";
```

### 3. Imports

```tsx
import '@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit/styles' // custom animations/keyframes
import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css'               // if using math rendering
```

### 4. Use

```tsx
import {
  ChatContainerRoot,
  ChatContainerContent,
  Message,
  MessageAvatar,
  MessageContent,
  MessageActions,
  MessageAction,
  PromptInput,
  PromptInputTextarea,
  PromptInputActions,
  PromptInputAction,
  ScrollButton,
  Markdown,
  CodeBlock,
  CodeBlockCode,
  CodeBlockGroup,
  CodeBlockCopyButton,
  Reasoning,
  ReasoningTrigger,
  ReasoningContent,
  Tool,
  Source,
  SourceTrigger,
  SourceContent,
  ThinkingBar,
  SystemMessage,
  FeedbackBar,
  Loader,
} from '@turbo-agent/daisyui-prompt-kit'
```

That's it. The library ships NO Tailwind CSS / DaisyUI — only ~3 KB of custom animations. All utility classes come from your own Tailwind build via `@source`.

---

## Composition Guidance

### Minimal Chat UI

```
ChatContainerRoot > ChatContainerContent > Message + PromptInput + ScrollButton
```

### Rich Assistant Message

```
Message > MessageAvatar + MessageContent(markdown) + MessageActions > MessageAction
           + CodeBlock + Source + FeedbackBar
```

### Tool-Calling Agent View

```
Message + Tool + Steps + Reasoning + ThinkingBar
```

### File-Enhanced Composer

```
FileUpload > FileUploadTrigger + FileUploadContent
  + PromptInput > PromptInputTextarea + PromptInputActions > PromptInputAction
```

---

## Built-in Icons Reference

These Lucide icons are used internally by library components. They require `@iconify/tailwind4` + `@iconify-json/lucide` to be configured in your CSS.

| Icon class | Used by |
|---|---|
| `icon-[lucide--copy]` | `CodeBlockCopyButton` |
| `icon-[lucide--check]` | `CodeBlockCopyButton` |
| `icon-[lucide--chevron-down]` | `ScrollButton`, `ReasoningTrigger`, `ChainOfThoughtTrigger`, `StepsTrigger`, `Tool` |
| `icon-[lucide--chevron-right]` | `ThinkingBar` |
| `icon-[lucide--thumbs-up]` | `FeedbackBar` |
| `icon-[lucide--thumbs-down]` | `FeedbackBar` |
| `icon-[lucide--x]` | `FeedbackBar` |
| `icon-[lucide--info]` | `SystemMessage` (info variant) |
| `icon-[lucide--circle-check-big]` | `SystemMessage` (success), `Tool` (output-available) |
| `icon-[lucide--triangle-alert]` | `SystemMessage` (warning) |
| `icon-[lucide--circle-alert]` | `SystemMessage` (error) |
| `icon-[lucide--loader-circle]` | `Tool` (input-streaming) |
| `icon-[lucide--settings-2]` | `Tool` (input-available, default) |
| `icon-[lucide--circle-x]` | `Tool` (output-error) |

Consumer-provided icons (used in demo pages but not built into components):

| Icon class | Typical usage |
|---|---|
| `icon-[lucide--arrow-up]` | Send button in PromptInput |
| `icon-[lucide--square]` | Stop button in PromptInput |
| `icon-[lucide--paperclip]` | File attach button |
| `icon-[lucide--cloud-upload]` | File drop zone |
| `icon-[lucide--search]`, `icon-[lucide--lightbulb]` | ChainOfThought step icons |

---

## Demo Project

The companion demo app runs on port `3005` and visually showcases every exported component with live examples.
